paper.title: Blue extreme disk-runaway stars with Gaia EDR3

extraction.summary: The paper studies 30 extreme disk-runaway stars with Gaia EDR3. It explicitly identifies 10 stars as gravitationally unbound or possibly unbound from the Milky Way: S5-HVS 1, HVS 5, HVS 6, B481, LAMOST HVS 1, LAMOST HVS 2, HVS 7, LAMOST HVS 4, J0136+2425, and HVS 8. The remaining 20 program stars are either bound or not claimed to be unbound.

method_chain.summary: Gaia EDR3 astrometry providing proper motions in right ascension and declination.

source_refs.context: spectrophotometric distances and radial velocities combined with Gaia EDR3 proper motions

method_chain.summary: Radial velocities from high-resolution spectra (MMT, UVES, FEROS, etc.) or literature values.

source_refs.context: radial velocities from published results and new spectral analyses

method_chain.summary: Atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, vsini) determined by fitting model spectra to observed spectra; stellar masses and ages from evolutionary tracks.

source_refs.context: model atmosphere fitting, SED fitting, and stellar parameter derivation

method_chain.summary: Spectrophotometric distances derived by combining surface gravity with angular diameter obtained from SED fitting.

source_refs.context: distance from surface gravity and angular diameter

method_chain.summary: Solar Galactocentric distance, circular velocity, and peculiar motion adopted from Schönrich et al. (2010). The paper does not quote explicit numeric values.

source_refs.context: uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest are treated analogously

parameters.name: z0
parameters.description: Height of Sun above the Galactic plane

source_refs.context: uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest are treated analogously
uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest are treated analogously
uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest are treated analogously
uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest are treated analogously
uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest are treated analogously
Solar position and motion from Schönrich et al. (2010)

method_chain.summary: Galactocentric Cartesian positions and velocities computed from observed coordinates, distances, proper motions, and radial velocities, using solar position and motion.

source_refs.context: combination of spectrophotometric distances, radial velocities, and proper motions to trace trajectories

method_chain.summary: Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang et al. (2013), a three-component model with bulge, disk, and dark matter halo.

source_refs.context: Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang et al. (2013)
Galactic potential model

method_chain.summary: Backwards orbit integration to the Galactic plane using a fourth-order Runge-Kutta-Fehlberg method with adaptive step size. One million Monte Carlo simulations propagate uncertainties in distance, radial velocity, proper motions, and solar parameters.

source_refs.context: numerical integration with Monte Carlo error propagation

method_chain.summary: Computation of Galactic rest-frame velocity, local escape velocity, the margin v_Grf - v_esc, and the bound probability P_b (fraction of Monte Carlo runs where the star is bound).

source_refs.context: definition of bound probability P_b as fraction of Monte Carlo runs bound

method_chain.summary: Ejection velocity (relative to rotating disk at plane crossing) and galactocentric radius at plane intersection derived from backwards orbits.

source_refs.context: derivation of ejection velocity and spatial origins

method_chain.summary: Classification of stars into GC (C), disk runaway (D), or outer rim (R) based on galactocentric radius at plane intersection r_p.

source_refs.context: flag in last column marks objects that potentially originate in the GC (C), are bona fide disk runaways (D), or stem from the outer rim of the disk (R)

inclusion_assessment.summary: S5-HVS1 is the most extreme star in the sample, gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way with P_b=0%, consistent with Hills ejection from the Galactic centre. Originally discovered by Koposov et al. (2020) and confirmed here with Gaia EDR3.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Explicitly stated to be the only object gravitationally unbound in Section 5.1 and P_b=0 in Table A.2.

source_refs.context: abstract: only three stars ... most notably S5-HVS1 ... gravitationally unbound
Within the error bars, our result for this object agrees with that of Koposov et al.(2020)...

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 5 is clearly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way with P_b=0%, and its origin is constrained to the solar neighbourhood, ruling out a GC origin. Originally from the MMT-HVS survey.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Explicitly stated as gravitationally unbound (line 176) and P_b=0 in Table A.2.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 6 is gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way with P_b=0%, and likely originates from the outer disk (r_p = 25 kpc). Originally from the MMT-HVS survey.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Explicitly stated as unbound (line 180: HVS 6 is unbound to the Milky Way) and P_b=0 in Table A.2.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B481 might be gravitationally unbound (P_b=60%), though the paper says it might be unbound. Originates from outer disk (r_p=29 kpc). Paper says "B481 and HVS 7 might be" unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states B481 might be unbound (line 180) and P_b=60% allows possibility.

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 1 is a hyper-runaway star (i.e., gravitationally unbound) with P_b=2%. Originally discovered by Huang et al. (2017). The paper confirms its unbound status.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Explicitly called hyper-runaway star (line 176) and P_b=2% indicates very likely unbound.

source_refs.context: LAMOST HVS 1 and LAMOST HVS 4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars.

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 2 is possibly unbound (hyper-runaway) depending on the Galactic mass model; P_b=66% implies bound in the adopted model but paper says its hyper-runaway status depends on model choice. Paper labels it as possibly unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states that whether it is hyper-runaway depends on model (line 177), and P_b=66% allows possibility.

source_refs.context: Whether LAMOST HVS 2 ... are hyper-runaway stars depends on the choice of the applied Galactic mass model.

candidate_groups_considered.description: All 30 program stars listed in Table 1 (Ejection velocity, galactocentric radius at plane intersection, and stellar mass).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper identifies a subset as gravitationally unbound or possibly unbound from the Milky Way. Stars that are bound or not explicitly claimed to be unbound are excluded. Included: S5-HVS 1, HVS 5, HVS 6, B481, LAMOST HVS 1, LAMOST HVS 2, HVS 7, LAMOST HVS 4, J0136+2425, HVS 8. Excluded: the remaining 20 stars.

source_refs.context: abstract describing sample and unbound stars
Table 1 caption and contents
discussion of unbound status of individual stars in Section 5.1, 5.2, 5.3
